# Binance.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the Binance REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports Spot, Margin, USD-M Futures, COIN-M Futures, and Options trading. Part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem providing a unified abstraction across 28+ cryptocurrency exchanges.

Binance.Net is the most widely adopted C# client for Binance, with full coverage of public and private endpoints, automatic WebSocket reconnection, comprehensive error handling via the `WebCallResult<T>` / `CallResult<T>` pattern, native AOT support, and built-in support for sub-accounts, brokerage, margin, and rebate operations. Current version: 12.x. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, basic usage examples, full release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/Binance.Net/): Full documentation hub including client reference, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/docs/ai-api-map.md): Quick intent-to-method map for routing common user requests to the correct client member
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with code patterns, endpoint map, common mistakes, and anti-hallucination rules

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/tree/master/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, fully runnable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, account balance, limit order placement, order status check
- [Futures Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): USD-M futures with leverage, market order, position retrieval, position close
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Ticker subscription, kline subscription, user data stream with proper teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): Using CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis for exchange-agnostic code
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): WebCallResult pattern, retry logic, common errors and their handling
- [Full Examples Repository](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/tree/master/Examples): Console, ASP.NET MVC, Blazor (server, WASM, client-side), WPF, Azure Functions, Minimal Web API

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net base library](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base abstractions used by Binance.Net and 28 other exchange libraries
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package providing unified access to all exchange libraries
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Binance.Net): Latest stable release on NuGet

- [Changelog](https://github.com/JKorf/Binance.Net/blob/master/Binance.Net/CHANGELOG.md): Version history
